headline: AI database deep dive: Oracle’s platform redesign
summary: Oracle has significantly redesigned its database architecture, integrating AI capabilities directly into its core offerings through its AI Database 26ai and Oracle Autonomous AI Lakehouse. These advancements aim to facilitate customer access to major AI models by connecting proprietary data, empowering semantic searches, and enhancing security with database-level privacy enforcement and quantum-safe encryption. TheCUBE's Oracle Data Deep Dive NYC event on April 15 will further explore how Oracle is embedding machine learning, vector search, and advanced analytics into its data platform to enable production-ready AI architectures.

headline: AMD Delivers Breakthrough MLPerf Inference 6.0 Results
summary: AMD's latest MLPerf Inference 6.0 submission shows significant advancements with its Instinct MI355X GPUs, surpassing 1 million tokens per second at multinode scale and expanding into new workloads like text-to-video generation. The results demonstrate competitive single-node performance against NVIDIA B200 and B300 GPUs, efficient scale-out, and broad ecosystem reproducibility, largely attributed to the AMD ROCm software stack. These achievements position AMD as a strong contender in the generative AI inference market, with a clear roadmap for future Instinct GPU series and rack-scale solutions.
label: negative
